R: It emphasises meeting present needs without compromising future generations. S: The UN Sustainable Development Goals outline specific targets.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on her friend for lying.
options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The workers have been ____ for a pay rise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a gantry girder, the lateral loads are resisted by:
options: The compression flange through lateral restraints
questions.solution: Gantry girders: lateral load to compression flange (surge load).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Glass used in windows is typically:
questions.solution: Soda-lime glass is the common window glass (cheap, easily workable).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 101.5 m. A foresight of 2.0 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The design bending strength of a laterally unsupported beam is:
questions.solution: Laterally unsupported beam: lateral-torsional buckling governs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laterite is an example of:
questions.solution: Wait: Laterite is actually a sedimentary/weathered rock, not igneous. Let me correct. Actually laterite is a weathered product of igneous rocks under tropical conditions, classified as a residual soil/sedimentary rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For fully developed laminar flow in a circular pipe, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Head loss is proportional to the mean velocity.
The maximum velocity equals twice the mean velocity.
The velocity profile is uniform across the section.
questions.solution: Laminar pipe flow has a parabolic velocity profile, not a uniform one — the other three statements are all true.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A prismatic compass measures bearings in the:
questions.solution: A prismatic compass reads whole circle bearings from 0° to 360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for yielding of steel (γm0) in IS 800 limit state design is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of most building stones is between:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy correction factor for laminar flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: α = 2.0 for laminar flow, ~1.05-1.10 for turbulent flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The local attraction error is detected when:
options: Fore bearing and back bearing differ by not exactly 180°
questions.solution: FB - BB ≠ 180° indicates local attraction (or errors).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a bolted connection, the minimum pitch of bolts is:
questions.solution: Min pitch = 2.5×nominal bolt diameter (IS 800). Max pitch = 16t or 200 mm (tension members).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most durable timber in India is:
questions.solution: Teak = most durable due to natural oils (resistant to termites/fungi).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Euler's equation of motion along a streamline is:
questions.solution: Euler: dp/ρ + v dv + g dz = 0 (inviscid flow along streamline).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 100.1 m. A foresight of 1.5 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The economical depth of a plate girder is approximately:
questions.solution: Economical depth ~ 1.1√(M/f_y t_w) or from optimization of weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plywood is made by bonding together:
options: Thin veneers of wood with alternating grain directions
questions.solution: Plywood = cross-laminated veneers for strength and stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 3 m under a head of 0.3 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 3 m, H = 0.3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times3\times0.3^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.9$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, if the backsight is 2.5 m and foresight is 1.2 m, the difference in elevation:
questions.solution: BS - FS = 2.5 - 1.2 = +1.3 → rise from instrument to foresight point.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A built-up section is used when:
options: Single rolled section is inadequate for the load
questions.solution: Built-up = fabricated from plates/sections for higher capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Asphalt and bitumen are:
questions.solution: Bitumen is viscoelastic - behaves elastically at low T, viscously at high T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy grade line lies above the hydraulic grade line by an amount equal to the:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chain surveying is suitable for:
questions.solution: Chain survey = simple, small, open, fairly level areas.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The buckling class for a rolled I-section column about its weak axis is:
questions.solution: Rolled I-section: major axis = class a, minor axis = class b (IS 800 Table 10).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The defect in timber where fibres separate due to excessive heat is called:
questions.solution: Check = longitudinal crack due to uneven drying/shrinkage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel of width 2 m, depth 2 m, slope 0.0005, Manning n = 0.014. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bolts subjected to both shear and tension are checked for:
questions.solution: An interaction (combined) check is required when bolts carry shear and tension together.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain energy stored per unit volume under axial stress σ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The correction for curvature and refraction in levelling is:
options: 0.0673 d² (d in km, answer in m)
questions.solution: Combined correction C_cr = 0.0673 d² m (d in km).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 10 m carries a point load of 15 kN at the free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = PL = 15×10 = 150 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a compass survey, the direction of a line is given by its:
questions.solution: Bearing = direction relative to N/S reference line.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A retaining wall of height 5 m retains soil with γ=16 kN/m³, φ=38°. The Rankine active thrust is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention period for a sedimentation tank is usually:
questions.solution: Typical detention time = 2-4 hours for plain sedimentation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The road density in India (km per 1000 km²) is approximately:
questions.solution: India's road density ~ 1.9 km/km² (among highest in the world).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 100 m. With back sight 1.2 m and fore sight 2.5 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modular ratio m for design of RC sections as per working stress method is:
questions.solution: m = 280/(3σ_cbc) as per IS 456 for WSM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries two equal 20 kN point loads placed 2 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 20 kN, a = 2 m, span = 5 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=20$ kN; $M_{max}=20\times2$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity distribution in turbulent flow through a pipe is:
questions.solution: Turbulent: log-law velocity profile (blunt, fuller than parabolic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 2.5 m at depth 2 m in soil with c=20 kPa, φ=32°, γ=19 kN/m³. (Nc=30, Nq=18, Ng=15). Net ultimate BC is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 4 m long carries a 8 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 8 × 4 = 32 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The BOD rate constant k at 20°C is typically:
questions.solution: k (base e) ~ 0.1-0.3/day at 20°C for domestic sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In Marshall mix design, the flow value is measured in:
questions.solution: Flow = vertical deformation at failure (2-4 mm for dense mixes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In levelling, the HI is 101.2 m. A foresight of 1.2 m is read on a staff. The reduced level of the point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of shear reinforcement along the length of a beam is:
questions.solution: Max spacing = 0.75d or 300 mm (whichever is less) for vertical stirrups.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 15 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 15 kN, a = 1 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=15$ kN; $M_{max}=15\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=15$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 6 m carries a UDL of 8 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 8×6²/2 = 144 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a consolidation test, the compression index Cc is the slope of:
options: e vs log σ' curve in the virgin compression region
log e vs log σ' curve
questions.solution: Cc = slope of e-logσ' plot in the normally consolidated range.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The critical section for shear in a flat slab is at:
questions.solution: Punching shear critical section at d/2 from column periphery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.4, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The effective depth of a beam is measured from:
options: Compression face to centroid of tension reinforcement
Centroid of tension steel to centroid of compression steel
Top of beam to bottom of beam
questions.solution: Effective depth d = distance from extreme compression fibre to centroid of tension reinforcement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 1 m is founded at depth 1.5 m. Soil: c=15 kPa, γ=18 kN/m³, Nc=25.1, Nq=12.7, Nγ=9.7. The ultimate bearing capacity (Terzaghi) is:
questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 806.7$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The minimum grade of concrete for mild exposure as per IS 456 is:
questions.solution: M20 minimum for reinforced concrete (mild exposure).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The consistency index of a soil with natural water content 30%, LL = 50%, PL = 20% is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the limit state method, the partial safety factor for concrete is:
questions.solution: γ_m = 1.5 for concrete, 1.15 for steel in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.68, e = 0.5, S = 0.75. The water content is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a falling head permeability test, the time required for the water level to fall from h₁ to h₂ depends on:
questions.solution: k = (aL/At) ln(h₁/h₂) where a = standpipe area, A = sample area, L = length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the same soil, the coefficient of curvature is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times3^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=67.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows at 2 m/s in a 0.3 m diameter pipe of length 1000 m, f = 0.025. The friction head loss is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 0 kPa and friction angle φ = 20°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 60 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 0 kPa, φ = 20°, σ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=0+60\times\tan 20^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=21.84$ kPa.

questions.stem: The modulus of rigidity (G) of a material is 80 GPa and its modulus of elasticity (E) is 200 GPa. The Poisson's ratio is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-jointed truss with m = 2j - 3 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Pitot tube is used to measure:
questions.solution: Pitot tube = velocity measurement device (stagnation minus static).

questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.7 and void ratio e = 0.7. The dry unit weight is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: According to Tresca's theory, yielding occurs when the maximum shear stress equals:
options: Half the yield stress in tension

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows through a pipe of diameter 0.3 m at 3 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a centrifugal pump, the manometric efficiency is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear stress in a Newtonian fluid is proportional to:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For subcritical flow, the Froude number is:
questions.solution: Subcritical: Fr < 1, supercritical: Fr > 1, critical: Fr = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is based on the conservation of:
questions.solution: Bernoulli's theorem expresses conservation of energy along a streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A floating body is stable if:
options: Metacentre is above centre of gravity
questions.solution: GM > 0 → M above G → stable. GM < 0 → unstable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.solution: A venturimeter measures discharge using the pressure difference between throat and inlet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 5 m wide carries 12 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 12 m³/s, B = 5 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{12}{5}=2.4$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{2.4^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.84$ m.

questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 10 m carries a UDL of 8 kN/m. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a UDL of 20 kN/m. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The elongation of a conical bar under its own weight W and length L is:
questions.solution: For a conical bar hanging under self-weight, δ = WL/(2AE) where A = area at top.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A column with slenderness ratio greater than a certain limit is classified as:
questions.solution: Slenderness ratio > critical value → long column (Euler buckling governs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 200 mm wide and 450 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slope at the free end of a cantilever of length L carrying a point load W at the free end is:
questions.solution: θ = ∫(M/EI)dx = WL²/2EI at free end of cantilever with end load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 120 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 1500 mm². The direct stress is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a balanced section with Fe415 steel, the limiting neutral axis depth factor xu,max/d is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies xu,max/d = 0.48 for Fe415 steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump occurs when flow changes from:
questions.solution: Hydraulic jump = transition from supercritical to subcritical (Fr₁ > 1 to Fr₂ < 1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of the plastic moment to the yield moment for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = M_p/M_y = S/Z = 1.5 for rectangle, 1.7 for circle, 1.14 for I-section.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's bearing capacity factors Nc, Nq, Nγ depend on:
questions.solution: Bearing capacity factors are functions of φ only.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of main bars in a slab is the smaller of:
questions.solution: Maximum spacing of main reinforcement is 3 times effective depth or 300 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The critical rate of superelevation for mixed traffic is:
options: Limited to avoid slow-moving vehicle sliding
questions.solution: With mixed traffic, e is limited by slow vehicles (max 7% plain, 10% hilly).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+80\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=57.3$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 500 mm, clear cover 20 mm and 20 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm.
